ground_truth.title: Rental Cars: 15 Tips for Better Rentals
ground_truth.author: Janice
ground_truth.main_content:
Renting a car on vacation is typically about getting safely and affordably from Point A to Point B (or Point C, too) with enough comfort and space for all passengers and baggage. However, when I rent, I am not immune to the appeal of a nice ride. An affordable convertible in Hawaii? Sign me up. Check out my 15 tips for raising the bar on car rentals, saving time, money, and hassle—and the quality of your vehicle below.

1. Determine a Baseline Cost: Visit online travel agency websites like Orbitz and Expedia, or car rental agency sites like Rentalcars.com, and look up rental car pricing for your trip destination and trip dates. Search for the most basic model you are willing to rent that also meets your passenger and baggage needs, and note the price. You can always upgrade later (often for less) at the rental counter.

2. Go to AutoSlash.com: AutoSlash.com is an online comparison platform that helps you find the best deals across car rental brokers and agencies. Unique to its quoting system, AutoSlash will ask you to input the specific credit card, insurance, or eligible member discounts, and applies publicly available and active coupons so that you no longer need to search for them.

3. Check Out Membership Sites: Several membership programs offer excellent car rental discounts. My favorite source is Costco Travel, which along with shopping and gas discounts, also provides access to discounted travel booking services, including car rentals. Surprisingly, you may find that the cost of a premium vehicle can sometimes run less than a basic economy rental.

4. Look for Coupons: To find rental car coupons online, go to RetailMeNot's Car Rental coupon page, where you will find pages and pages of deals and discount codes. Once you have found the best discount code go directly to the car rental website of your choice, like Hertz, Avis, etc., to input the code to see if the savings are better.

5. Know About Turo & Other Car Rental Alternatives: Recognized as the Airbnb of the rental car market, Turo connects private car owners who want to convert their underused cars into cash and travelers who would like to drive them. Turo's rates tend to run less than rental car companies—and in some cases, significantly less. Other alternatives include Zipcar, Getaround, Lyft rentals, Kyte, and Audi On Demand.

6. Avoid the Airport if You Can: Another excellent money-saving tip includes avoiding renting from the airport when time and patience allow. Instead, rent your car in town. The same one-way rental picked up at the airport would have cost $200 more in a Boston example.

7. Determine if You Need Rental Car Insurance: It is a rare traveler who is not confused about rental car insurance. Learn when you need to buy rental car insurance or when you can skip it and save.

8. Skip Booking With Discount Agencies: I recommend avoiding booking rental cars with discount car rental agency brands whenever possible because the trade-off of savings for increased counter wait times and hassle is generally not worth it.

9. Consider One-Way Rentals: Car rental rates often cost less when a renter returns the car to the original rental office instead of another destination. However, if I can save money on airfare by buying an open jaw ticket—arriving in one destination and leaving from another—I will often rent a car in one city and return it to another.

10. Negotiate for an Upgrade at the Counter: Most of the time rental car company counter representatives will be willing to negotiate on the price of an upgrade depending on the cars they have in stock. Be informed, be flexible, and be friendly when negotiating.

11. Capture Photos or Video of the Rental: Take photos of the entire exterior of your rental car before you drive it off the lot. Your photos or video give you time and date-stamped evidence of the pre-driving condition of your rental car.

12. Fill Up Before Returning the Car: Avoid paying the rental car company's higher gas prices by filling up before returning the car.

13. Inspect the Car for Damage: Before signing off, do a walk-around inspection and take photos of any existing damage.

14. Check All the Rental Car Company's Fees: Review all fees including additional driver fees, airport concession fees, and one-way fees.

15. Sign Up for the Company's Loyalty Program: Enrolling in a rental car company's loyalty program can earn you free upgrades, faster service, and other perks.
with: AutoSlash.com is my second stop for getting a great car rental deal. It is an online comparison platform that helps you find the best deals across car rental brokers and agencies.

My favorite source for membership car rental discounts is Costco Travel, which along with shopping and gas discounts, also provides access to discounted travel booking services, including car rentals.
Recognized as the Airbnb of the rental car market, Turo connects private car owners who want to convert their underused cars into cash and travelers who would like to drive them.
Avoid renting from the airport when time and patience allow. Instead, rent your car in town. The same one-way rental picked up at the airport would have cost $200 more.

One of my favorite affordable ways to upgrade my rental car is at the rental counter. Most of the time rental car company counter representatives will be willing to negotiate on the price of an upgrade.
